blocks.html:
Claude improves fastest on feedback that is concrete and executable. "Handle edge cases better" gives the model nothing to locate. "For input X, the expected key service_visits is missing because the source says 'maintenance entries' — here's the excerpt" lets it fix exactly that. The iteration loop for code: define behavior with tests, ask for the smallest useful implementation, run the tests, feed back exact failures, and fix one failure class at a time.

When the same defect recurs across runs of the same prompt, stop retrying — that's a signal the prompt or schema needs a structural change (a few-shot example, a split tool, a new field). Structural fixes generalize; retries don't. And when requirements are genuinely uncertain (caching, auth, consistency), have Claude interview you and surface the decisions before implementing.

A code review agent floods PRs with noise. What it needs isn't a bigger model — it's explicit criteria: report bugs, security, data loss, missing tests, breaking API changes; skip style nits and speculative performance advice. To cut false positives, show 2–3 examples of acceptable patterns next to genuinely problematic ones — far more effective than "be conservative." And when developers dismiss findings, capture detected_pattern / rule_id / evidence fields so dismiss rates can be aggregated by pattern and the noisy patterns suppressed. Dismissals are feedback, not noise.

Evaluation discipline: segment results by document type, field, prompt version, model, source quality, confidence band, and correction category. Aggregate accuracy misleads — 97% overall can hide a critical field at 80%. Watch for the low-value generated test smells: asserting only that code doesn't throw, duplicating coverage, ignoring fixtures, testing implementation instead of behavior, missing error paths — and fix them by documenting test standards with contrasting examples. Finally, resist infrastructure-first responses: before building validation microservices, try the cheap high-leverage fixes (examples, criteria, schema changes) that usually solve recurring patterns.

items: Feedback = failing input + expected vs actual + the trigger. Vague requests produce vague fixes.
Recurring defects demand structural prompt/schema changes, not retries.
Review agents need explicit report/skip criteria and contrasting few-shot examples.
Instrument dismissals (pattern, rule, evidence) to turn them into improvements.
Segment every evaluation — aggregates hide localized failure.
